rationale_top3: Entertainment facilities are exceptional with a highly-praised water park (Sammy's Water Splash Park), heated pools including dedicated toddler area, jumping pillow, arcade games room, kids club activities, and direct beach access keeping children entertained throughout their stay. Cleanliness standards are outstanding with reviews describing it as 'the cleanest park we have ever stayed in' featuring spotlessly clean amenities, immaculate cabins, child-sized bathrooms, and staff constantly maintaining grounds throughout the day. Beachfront location provides direct access to the protected Easts Beach inlet for safe swimming, stunning coastal walks to blowholes and lighthouse, creek with wildlife, and ocean views from premium accommodations, all contributing to a memorable family coastal holiday.
rationale_honourable: The park demonstrates exceptional customer loyalty with families returning annually for 3-40 years, generous site sizes accommodating large caravans with room for annexes, premium ensuite sites described as 'next level,' and overwhelmingly friendly and welcoming staff who enhance the overall experience.
water_fun: Heated pools, splash park, beachfront swimming
kids_play: Water park, jumping pillow, arcade, kids club
pet_detail: Pets not permitted; one reviewer specifically noted disappointment about not being able to bring their 'fur baby'.
best_for: This park is best for families with children aged 2-12 who prioritize extensive kids' entertainment facilities, water play options, and immaculate cleanliness in a beachfront setting.
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Easts Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 88/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-25
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 288

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (19/20) — Reviews overwhelmingly praise the "incredible water park," heated pools with dedicated toddler area (70cm deep), jumping pillow, games room with arcade (Pac-Man, Space Invaders), kids club activities (making slime, rice bubble slices, crafts), 5:30pm movies, kids disco, and direct beach access. Families report kids "never wanted to leave" and facilities keep children entertained without leaving the park.

Nature (18/20) — The park sits directly on Easts Beach with a "protected & private inlet" for safe swimming. Reviews highlight the "breathtaking" coastal walks to little blowhole, lighthouse, and Gerringong, creek running through park with ducks, "stunning" ocean views from beachfront cabins, and proximity to Saddleback Mountain lookout. Some note limited natural shade and the developed/resort-style environment reduces bushland feel.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Described as "the cleanest park we have ever stayed in" with "spotlessly clean" amenities, "immaculate" cabins, and staff constantly maintaining grounds and emptying bins "many times a day." Child-sized bathrooms praised as brilliant. Two negative reviews from peak periods noted temporary cleanliness lapses and pool odour issues, preventing a perfect score.

Value (12/15) — Pricing runs $80-$169+ per night, which many call "expensive" or "not cheap," but most families conclude it's "worth every single penny" for the facilities provided. Several reviews note it's pricier than standard parks but "you see why when you get there." Some older guests felt poor value if not using kids facilities.

Site Size (9/10) — Powered sites described as "very generous in size" with room for "tent, gazebo and car" or "9m long caravan." Ensuite sites praised as spacious with large bathroom and storage cupboard. A few reviews note sites can be "a little tight for larger caravans" and some sites close together, particularly tent sites.

Sentiment (9/10) — Exceptionally high repeat visitation (3rd-4th stays common, one family visiting 20+ years, another 40 years). Families frequently report rebooking immediately and making it "an annual trip." Overwhelming recommendation rate with phrases like "can't wait to be back" and "best holiday park we have been to." Few negative experiences centered on booking errors, noise, or unwelcoming staff interactions.

Location (7/10) — Beachfront position is outstanding, but park is 5-10 minutes drive from Kiama town center (not walkable). Close to Jamberoo Action Park, blowholes, and coastal attractions. Train line proximity causes noise on some sites (coal trains at 3:50am, 4:40am, 5:50am noted). Reviewers appreciate the secluded feel while being close enough to town amenities.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Train line noise significantly impacts sites at the back of the park, with multiple reviews citing very loud coal trains at 3:50am, 4:40am, and 5:50am disturbing sleep.
2. Premium pricing ($80-$169+/night) reduces value score, with several reviews calling it "expensive" and poor value for guests not using kids facilities or traveling off-peak.
3. During peak periods (Christmas/holidays), cleanliness standards dropped with "disgusting" amenities reported, fire alarm incidents, and pool odour problems, plus booking/cancellation issues frustrated some long-term customers.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"cleanest park we have ever stayed in," "water park, jumping pillow, pool and beach," "kids loved all the facilities," "spotlessly clean amenities," "best caravan park in Australia," "ensuite facilities were the best you'll see," "worth every single penny," "right on the beach," "staff are incredibly friendly and welcoming," "amazing kids bathroom," "far too expensive for us," "train line very close and coal trains very loud at night"

rationale_top3: Outstanding natural environment with stunning ocean views, positioned between two beaches with direct access to the Kiama coastal walk - a perfect setting for families who value coastal scenery and outdoor exploration over structured entertainment programs. Exceptional cleanliness standards with amenities consistently described as 'spotless' and 'immaculately clean,' plus one of the best camp kitchens seen by traveling families, though recent 2026 reviews show some inconsistency in cabin cleaning. Perfect location within walking distance of two beaches, 10 minutes to Kiama town centre, and easy access to the blowhole, markets, shops and cafes - consistently praised as 'unbeatable' and keeping families returning for 3+ consecutive years.
rationale_honourable: Surf Beach Holiday Park delivers an outstanding coastal experience with spectacular ocean views and direct access to two beautiful beaches. Families consistently praise the immaculate facilities, particularly the exceptional camp kitchen and spotless amenities with thoughtful touches like bath mats. While on-site kids entertainment is limited to a playground, the location's proximity to beaches, coastal walks, and Kiama town centre provides abundant natural entertainment that keeps families returning year after year.
water_fun: Pool with ocean lookout, beach 800m walk
kids_play: Playground available, close to beaches and walks
pet_detail: Pet-friendly cabins available with fenced decks; beaches not dog-friendly but coastal walking paths welcome dogs with off-leash areas nearby
best_for: Families with young children who love beach holidays and outdoor exploration, particularly those who value natural environment over structured kids clubs and don't mind walking to attractions.
executive_summary:
PARK: Surf Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 76/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-04-30
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 71

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (12/20) — Reviews consistently mention a playground that "kids loved" and proximity to beaches and coastal walks, but explicitly note "not a significant amount for young kids to do" on-site. No evidence of kids clubs, organized activities, or water entertainment beyond the pool. Families rely heavily on nearby beaches and walking tracks for entertainment.

Nature (19/20) — Outstanding natural environment with "stunning ocean views," positioned between two beaches (Surf Beach and Kendalls Beach), direct access to the Kiama coastal walk, and proximity to Minnamurra rainforest. Multiple reviews praise "gorgeous location with many exits straight out to lovely grassed areas and the beautiful beach" and "walk for miles from there."

Cleanliness (14/15) — Exceptional cleanliness with reviews calling amenities "always spotless," "immaculately clean," and "the cleanest I have ever seen on a caravan park." However, recent 2026 review cited "cleaning standards inside the cabin were below par" and 2024 review mentioned "hygienic standards were slightly below par," suggesting some inconsistency.

Value (11/15) — Mixed feedback on value with comments that it's "a bit pricey" and one family felt supplies were insufficient for a weekend stay. However, others found "excellent value" and "good price." The council-run facility offers various accommodation options "priced accordingly."

Site Size (7/10) — Caravan sites described as "big" and "very spacious," but multiple reviews warn it's "little bit tight for larger vans," "tight access to caravan sites," and "parking spaces are pretty tight." Manoeuvring challenges noted frequently, though concrete slabs appreciated.

Sentiment (9/10) — Overwhelmingly positive with multiple families returning for 3+ consecutive years. Phrases like "will definitely be back," "can't wait to come back," and "we even extended our stay" common. One serious security concern (bike theft, 1-star review) and occasional service complaint prevent perfect score.

Location (10/10) — Perfect location score with "walking distance from two beaches," "10 min easy walk" to Kiama town centre, easy access to blowhole, markets, shops, and cafes. Consistently praised as "unbeatable" and "close to everything."

WHY NOT 100:
1. Limited on-site kids entertainment beyond a basic playground - families with young children specifically noted there's "not a significant amount for young kids to do" on-site, requiring reliance on beaches and external attractions.
2. Security and maintenance concerns emerged in recent reviews including one serious theft incident with non-functioning security booms/cameras, plus inconsistent cabin cleaning standards in 2026 reviews after years of perfect cleanliness ratings.
3. Site accessibility challenges for larger rigs with multiple warnings about "tight access," narrow roads, and difficult maneuvering that can cause stress during busy periods, limiting appeal for families with large caravans.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Kids loved the playground and being so close to the water", "amenities were always spotless", "one of the best camp kitchens we have seen while travelling", "Not a significant amount for young kids to do", "some of the cabins are looking a little tired and it is a bit pricey", "We have stayed here 3 years in a row", "tight access to caravan sites", "stunning location"

park_name: Kendalls On The Beach Holiday Park
rationale_top3: Exceptional beachfront location with absolute beach frontage on beautiful Kendalls Beach and easy walking access to Kiama town (15-20 minutes), consistently described as prime position with stunning coastal views and walks Outstanding natural environment scoring 19/20 with gorgeous quiet beach, beautifully leafy surroundings, and extensive foreshore walks, though waves can be challenging for very young children Strong cleanliness standards with amenities frequently described as spotless, immaculately clean, and 5-star quality, though some recent reviews noted facilities could use updating
rationale_honourable: Exceptional guest loyalty demonstrated by families returning for their 21st consecutive year and consistently rebooking, with management team praised for being friendly and welcoming and going out of their way to assist guests
water_fun: Beach frontage only, no pool facilities
kids_play: Public playground nearby with slides and swings
pet_detail: Dog friendly park with pets genuinely welcome, though beach itself is not dog friendly with small off-leash area on headland available
best_for: Families seeking a beach-focused holiday with older kids who can enjoy the surf, coastal walks, and easy access to Kiama's attractions without needing on-site entertainment facilities
executive_summary:
PARK: Kendalls On The Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-03-25
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 87

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (10/20) — Reviews indicate no on-site kids entertainment facilities or activities program. Families mention a "public children's play park that has a slide and swings not too far from the beach" and describe it as "great for young families" with the beach itself being the main entertainment. Multiple reviews note "perfect for all aged kids with walks, beach and playground" but entertainment is entirely nature-based rather than organized activities.

Nature (19/20) — Exceptional natural environment scores with consistent praise for "right on the beautiful Kendall's beach," "absolute beach frontage," "stunning quiet beach," and "gorgeous beach, great location, lots of walks and places to see." The park is described as "beautifully leafy and incredibly relaxing" with "lovely beach and great walks around the foreshore." Only minor deduction for one review noting "Beach is nice although not good for young kids with waves crashing right in beach."

Cleanliness (14/15) — Strong cleanliness ratings with frequent mentions of "amenities are spotless," "immaculately clean," "5 star amenities," and "always clean and well maintained." Recent reviews consistently praise "clean amenities" and "immaculate" facilities. Minor points lost for a Feb 2026 review noting "amenities were only moderately clean" and Feb 2026 comment that "toilets and bathrooms could do with a spruce up."

Value (11/15) — Mixed value perception with some noting "$45 for 2 of us to stay on an unpowered site, which is great considering the location" while others comment "a little bit expensive" and "$60pn for a powered site which is up there." Several reviews mention pricing concerns, with one noting "all sites are the same price ($60) regardless of whether your site has torn matting, is uneven and your view is the row of 12 bins." The beachfront location justifies pricing for most but not universally perceived as excellent value.

Site Size (7/10) — Moderate site size scores with mixed feedback. Positive mentions include "bigger than usual pitches," "big sites with heaps of room," and "good size." However, multiple concerns noted: "camp sites are a little crowded," "everyone too close to each other," "spot 41, which was so close to number 40 that our slide-out was under their awning," "only small, very narrow to get in," and "camp has lots of cabins and only 15 camp-sites." Space adequate but not exceptional.

Sentiment (9/10) — Very positive overall sentiment with exceptional loyalty shown by "21st year at Kendall's," "past 4 years" annual visits, and "rebooked for 2026." Frequent mentions of "will definitely stay again," "we'll be back," and "would definitely return." Strong praise for staff being "friendly and welcoming" and "management team went out of their way." Points lost due to several negative experiences with COVID policy, neighbour noise complaints, and management described as "a bit of a dragon."

Location (10/10) — Perfect location scores with "prime position," "excellent location," "walking distance into town" (15-20 minute walk), "easy walk into Kiama," and "close drive to Blow hole and main shops." Described as "immaculately located within the city centre of Kiama" with "walking track that takes you straight to Kiama shops." Direct beach access is the standout feature mentioned in virtually every review.

WHY NOT 100:
1. No on-site kids entertainment or activity programs - families rely entirely on natural environment and must create their own entertainment, with no pool, waterpark, jumping pillow, games room, or organized activities that Gold-rated parks typically offer.

2. Significant noise disturbance from neighbouring properties - multiple recent reviews (2026, 2025) detail serious issues with "extremely loud music across the entire campground all day long" from adjacent residents, with one reviewer stating "this completely ruined what could have been a peaceful weekend" and management appearing unable to resolve the issue.

3. Cramped site spacing and inconsistent site quality - several reviews note sites are "a little crowded," "too close to each other," with specific complaints about sites 41, 49, 60, and 61 being substandard, plus the park charging uniform pricing despite significant quality differences between sites.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"perfect family holiday park", "21st year at Kendall's", "right on the beautiful Kendall's beach", "great for young families", "playground for young kids", "amenities are spotless", "loud music from neighbours", "Beach is nice although not good for young kids with waves", "camp sites are a little crowded", "absolute beach frontage", "whole park is child friendly", "management urgently needs to find a solution with the neighbors"

park_name: Werri Beach Holiday Park Gerringong [Kiama Coast Holiday Park]
rationale_top3: Outstanding beachfront location directly opposite Werri Beach with exceptional natural environment, ocean views, walking tracks, rock pools and coastal scenery consistently praised across reviews Good site sizes with large, flat, spacious grassed areas suitable for larger rigs, plus excellent location access to local amenities, bowling club and nearby Kiama Strong positive sentiment with friendly, helpful staff (Jackie, Dave and Jade mentioned by name) and high repeat visitor rate among satisfied guests
rationale_honourable: Werri Beach Holiday Park earns Silver recognition primarily for its unbeatable beachfront location directly across from Werri Beach and excellent natural environment access. While the park delivers on location and beach proximity with consistently positive staff interactions, it's held back by variable cleanliness standards in some cabins, limited on-site kids entertainment facilities, and concerns about value given pricing inconsistencies and maintenance issues in some accommodation units.
water_fun: Pool available, occasional maintenance issues reported
kids_play: Limited on-site entertainment, beach is main attraction
pet_detail: Dog friendly cabins available with fenced yards, off-leash area on-site, dog beach access 1km walk away
best_for: Best for families with pets who want direct beach access and natural coastal surroundings, and who are comfortable with basic facilities and variable cabin quality
executive_summary:
PARK: Werri Beach Holiday Park Gerringong [Kiama Coast Holiday Park]
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2025
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 75

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (10/20) — Very limited on-site kids entertainment mentioned; one review noted pool available and kids using it even in rain, one mentioned lack of full tennis court, but no playground, kids club, or organized activities referenced. Main entertainment is the beach itself.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural environment with park positioned directly across from Werri Beach with ocean views, walking tracks along esplanade, nearby ocean swimming pools and rock pools. Reviews consistently praise "incredible beach," "rolling green hills meet the ocean," and beautiful coastal scenery.

Cleanliness (10/15) — Mixed results with many praising "spotless amenities," "super clean toilet buildings," and well-maintained grounds, but significant negative reviews about "filthy cabin floors," "sand/dirt all over the floor," "dirty bugs over kitchen benches," "spa bath was filthy," and ants in beds. Amenities generally clean but cabin standards vary considerably.

Value (11/15) — Mixed value perceptions with several complaints about pricing: "$280 per night for three to three and half star quality," "Highway robbery for $60 pn in a paddock," "$220 dollars a night this is a ripp off," and "unpowered sites were very expensive at $55." Some found it "very good value" compared to Kiama, but consensus suggests pricing doesn't always match quality delivered.

Site Size (8/10) — Generally positive feedback on site size with reviews noting "large, flat site," "big caravan sites," "good sized grassed sites perfect for our large Oz tent," "plenty of space," and "spacious" sites. Some noted sites are "narrow but long" and adequate for larger rigs.

Sentiment (8/10) — Strong positive sentiment with many return visitors and recommendations, excellent staff praise ("Jackie," "Dave and Jade" mentioned positively), but dampened by several very disappointed 1-star reviews about cleanliness, check-in issues, and unmet expectations. High repeat booking intention among satisfied guests.

Location (10/10) — Outstanding location scores with park directly opposite beach, walking distance to rock pools, close to bowling club, shops 5 minutes away, short drive to Kiama, and easy access to Gerringong town. Consistently described as "perfect location," "couldn't be better," and "amazing location."

WHY NOT 100:
1. Inconsistent cabin cleanliness with multiple reports of dirty floors, filthy spa baths, ants, unwashed surfaces, and poor pre-arrival preparation despite charging premium prices up to $280/night.
2. Virtually no on-site kids entertainment infrastructure beyond a basic pool (which has had maintenance issues), with no playground, kids club, jumping pillow, or organized activities mentioned across 75 reviews.
3. Value concerns with pricing perceived as too high relative to quality delivered, particularly for older-style facilities and basic amenities that several reviewers compared unfavorably to other parks and accommodation options in the area.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"perfect location just across the road from an incredible beach", "cabin floors where filthy", "spotless amenities", "Staff are super friendly", "$280 per night for three to three and half star quality", "large, flat site which is great", "lovely little family-friendly, dog-friendly gem", "Highway robbery for $60 pn in a paddock"

rationale_top3: Exceptional natural setting with direct beach access (1 minute walk) and riverside positioning creates an outstanding coastal location for families, with the sound of waves and beautiful surroundings being consistently praised across reviews. Good range of family entertainment including dual swimming pools, jumping pillow, playgrounds, and the famous talking cockatoo, plus animal enclosures with chickens, ducks, and rabbits that delight children, though organized programming is limited. Strong location advantage with proximity to Gerroa, Berry, Kiama, and Wollongong, plus access to the Kiama coastal walk, making it an excellent base for South Coast exploration.
rationale_honourable: Seven Mile Beach Holiday Park offers an exceptional beachfront location with direct access to beautiful Seven Mile Beach and a peaceful riverside setting. Families appreciate the dual pool facilities, jumping pillow, and the beloved talking cockatoo that delights children. While the park shows promise with recent management improvements and excellent natural surroundings, inconsistent maintenance standards—particularly in amenities cleanliness—and limited site sizes prevent it from achieving a higher rating.
water_fun: Two pools, beach access, kayak hire available
kids_play: Jumping pillow, playgrounds, talking cockatoo, animal enclosure
pet_detail: Pet friendly except during school holidays; dogs must be on leash at all times and not allowed in pool areas, on beach, or in surrounding bush/river areas.
best_for: Budget-conscious families with young children who want beach proximity and basic entertainment facilities and can overlook inconsistent maintenance standards.
executive_summary:
PARK: Seven Mile Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 62/100
DATE SCORED: 2024-01-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 68

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (14/20) — Families consistently praise the jumping pillow, dual swimming pools, playgrounds, and the famous talking cockatoo that brings joy to children. Animal enclosures with chickens, ducks, and rabbits provide entertainment, and kayak hire is available for river exploration. However, one major review noted "only one holiday activity for the children" during a week-long school holiday stay, indicating limited organized programming compared to other parks.

Nature (18/20) — The park's standout feature is its exceptional natural setting with direct beach access ("1 minute walk to the beach"), positioning alongside Crooked River for kayaking, and beautiful coastal surroundings. Reviews highlight "listening to the waves crashing as you fall asleep" and "beautiful view of the river." The Kiama coastal walk is accessible by bus from the park entrance. Minor deductions for the riverside being described as "stagnant creek" in some reviews and dogs not being allowed on surrounding beach or bush areas.

Cleanliness (7/15) — This category shows the widest variation in reviews, indicating highly inconsistent standards. Recent positive reviews note "spotless amenities," "the cleanest it has ever been," and staff "constantly checking that all is well." However, multiple reviews across 2024-2025 describe facilities as "absolutely disgraceful," "filthiest I have experienced anywhere," with specific mentions of "mould in the shower, cobwebs everywhere," "toilets full," and "bins overflowing." The beachside appears better maintained than riverside.

Value (8/15) — Mixed value perception with some praising "fantastic prices" and "great value for money," while others found it "very pricey for the location and size" at "$50 a night" or "$795 for 2 nights" in premium cabins. Multiple reviews mention the park is "overpriced" given maintenance issues. The closed cafe/shop reduces convenience value. Council-run park pricing doesn't always match facility standards.

Site Size (6/10) — Consistent feedback that "powered sites are a bit on the small side" with "sites are very tight" for larger caravans. Reviews specifically mention difficulty maneuvering bigger rigs and recommend notifying reception about large vehicles. Ground is "really uneven with quite deep hollows" causing flooding issues. Some sites described as "dirt patch" that becomes muddy in rain. Concrete slabs with mesh are appreciated where available.

Sentiment (5/10) — Deeply polarized guest sentiment reflecting the park's inconsistency. Loyal returning families ("9 years," "27 years") show strong attachment, with some calling it "one of our favourite family holidays." However, numerous recent reviews express disappointment about decline: "has gone down hill," "we will never ever return," "can't see us going back." Management changes appear to have caused upheaval. Repeat visitation is split between devoted regulars and those who won't return.

Location (9/10) — Outstanding location scores with "perfect location," "great location by the ocean and river," and proximity to Gerroa, Berry, Kiama, and Wollongong. Beach access is the primary asset ("just across the road"). The main safety concern is road noise and danger with children needing to cross the road between park sections. Close to Fisherman's Club for dining. Well-positioned for South Coast exploration.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Cleanliness standards are severely inconsistent, with multiple 2024-2025 reviews describing "filthiest" and "disgraceful" amenities including mould, overflowing bins, and facilities not cleaned for days—this is the park's most critical failure for family safety and comfort.
2. Site sizes are inadequate for modern caravans with uneven ground causing flooding issues ("annexe floor filled up with water about 5cm deep"), and the riverside section is particularly run-down with "overgrown grass lots of dirt" and "zero internet."
3. Value proposition is undermined by high pricing relative to facility standards, with premium cabins showing poor maintenance (mould, broken appliances, insufficient inventory) and minimal organized activities during peak periods when families need them most.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spotless amenities, great hot shower," "amenities were absolutely disgraceful," "jumping pillow and talking cockatoo," "1 minute walk to the beach," "park has gone down hill," "cleanest it has ever been," "sites are a bit on the small side," "one of our favourite family holidays," "filthiest I have experienced anywhere," "beautiful location with beach, river and pool," "overpriced and an allround very upsetting disappointing experience," "perfect place to bring the kids"
